question_text: A student compares two light bulbs: a 60 W incandescent bulb with 5% efficiency and a 12 W LED bulb with 85% efficiency. Calculate how much energy each bulb converts into useful light per second, and explain why the LED is considered more efficient.
correct_answer: Incandescent: 3 J/s of light; LED: 10.2 J/s of light. The LED converts much more of its input energy into useful light, wasting less as heat.
explanation: Useful light energy = Efficiency × Power. Incandescent: 5% × 60 = 3 J/s; LED: 85% × 12 = 10.2 J/s. Despite using only 20% of the power, the LED produces more than three times the useful light because it wastes far less energy as heat.
